market.advice: Breadth stayed healthy into the close: 67% of stocks sit above their 50-day MA, VIX is calm and falling at 16.1, and SPY closed at $747.71 (+0.9% on the week) above its rising 50-day ($737.57). This remains a financials-healthcare-utilities-REIT-led advance -- custody and money-center banks (BK, NTRS, STT, BAC), healthcare (LLY, CVS, ELV, LQDA), data-center-power utilities (D, AEP, DTE) and diversified REITs (SPG, PSA, TRNO, IIPR) dominate the cleanest setups. The one notable divergence: QQQ is still below its 50-day even as SPY leads, and the held semiconductor book (NVDA, TSM, LRCX, MU, AMAT) is drawing SELL or NEUTRAL reads from TradingView despite strong quant scores -- chip-sector weakness ('chip sector takes it on the chin' per overnight headlines, with traders positioning for an eventual Nvidia rally) is the one real crack under an otherwise healthy tape. TradingView's independent read agrees with most of tonight's leaders, but flags real dissent on Caterpillar (CAT) -- an outright SELL on TV against a 92.1 quant score -- and several extended names (OKTA RSI 84.1, SIRI RSI 89.2) that are still technically confirmed but due a pause. Geopolitical risk remains the tail hazard: the Strait of Hormuz threat level was raised to 'severe' after tanker strikes, Iran-US tension continues around Khamenei's funeral, and Saudi Arabia cut crude prices -- none of this is showing up in VIX yet, but it is the most likely source of a sudden risk-off gap. A wide earnings catalyst window (7-30 days) is open across nearly every financial and healthcare pick on this list -- expect volatility to pick up name-by-name as those prints land. Normal position sizing is appropriate given the low-VIX, broad-breadth backdrop, but favor the less-extended STRONG BUY names (RL, GRMN, PKG) over the more stretched ones (SIRI, OKTA, BAC) if adding fresh capital.

picks.name: Liquidia Corporation
picks.thesis: The highest-scored setup in the entire scan (94.8): a rare-disease pharma breaking to a fresh 52-week high on a 4-contraction VCP (tightest 0.5%) with the RS line confirming new leadership, and cheap at 14.6x forward earnings against explosive post-launch revenue growth.
strengths: Top combined score in the scan, cleanest VCP (tightest contraction just 0.5%)
RS line at new highs with a volume dry-up into the base -- textbook accumulation
Revenue up over 4,000% YoY off the Yutrepia launch base; 14.6x forward P/E is inexpensive for the growth
risks: Small-cap biotech ($7.2B) -- single-product concentration risk; earnings in 36 days is a real gap-risk window later
16.2% short interest / 6.2 days-to-cover -- can cut both ways (squeeze fuel or air-pocket on any miss)
watch: TV agrees emphatically (16 buy/1 sell) -- confirmation, not contradiction; size normally into the breakout, trim ahead of the Aug 12 print

picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company
picks.thesis: Mega-cap GLP-1 leader at a fresh high with EPS +170% YoY -- the single largest fundamental acceleration of any large-cap in the scan, and TV's strongest possible read confirms it.
strengths: EPS growth ~170% YoY, revenue +55.5% -- unmatched scale + growth combination
TV STRONG BUY unanimous on the buy side (16 buy/0 sell)
Sits at new highs with RS confirming institutional leadership
risks: 27.8x forward P/E already prices in a lot of the growth
Earnings in 29 days -- GLP-1 competitive/pricing headlines move this stock sharply
watch: Best behaves as a core long-term holding; add on any base, not on a spike day

picks.name: CVS Health Corporation
picks.thesis: A cheap turnaround story (12.4x forward P/E) with EPS +63% YoY breaking out with RS at new highs -- TV confirms with a clean BUY read.
strengths: EPS +63.1%, revenue +6.1% -- margin recovery finally showing up in the numbers
RS at new highs, VCP intact, cheapest forward multiple among the healthcare picks
TV BUY (15/2/9) backs the technical read
risks: Earnings in 29 days
Managed-care/PBM regulatory headline risk is never fully off the table for this name
watch: Clean, un-extended entry -- good risk/reward relative to the more stretched healthcare names

picks.name: Elevance Health
picks.thesis: Managed-care peer to CVS with a cleaner TV read (STRONG BUY, 15/1/10) despite negative trailing EPS growth -- the market is pricing in a medical-cost-ratio inflection, not trailing numbers.
strengths: TV STRONG BUY, one of the most lopsided buy/sell ratios on the board
RS at new highs, VCP confirmed
14.3x forward P/E is inexpensive versus the sector
risks: Trailing EPS growth is -16.8% -- this is a forward-multiple, not trailing-earnings, story
Earnings in just 8 days -- tight binary-event window
watch: Consider trimming into strength ahead of the print given how close earnings are

picks.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
picks.thesis: One of the cleanest setups in the whole scan: a textbook 3-contraction VCP (tightest 2.5%) breaking to new highs, cheap at 14.2x forward earnings against 41.8% EPS growth.
strengths: Score 94.2, near-top of the entire universe
RS line at new highs -- institutions leading, not lagging
14.2x forward P/E vs 41.8% EPS growth and 13.4% revenue growth
risks: Earnings in 8 days -- gap risk either direction
TradingView could not resolve a rating for this symbol -- no independent cross-check here, size accordingly
watch: Let earnings pass or confirm the breakout holds before adding size

picks.name: Northern Trust Corporation
picks.thesis: Custody-bank peer to BK with the same rate-and-AUM tailwind, RS at new highs, and TV confirmation (15 buy/2 sell).
strengths: EPS +42.6%, revenue +13.9% -- mirrors BK's acceleration
RS at new highs, tight VCP base
TV BUY backs the setup
risks: Earnings in 15 days
Custody-bank group is now crowded -- BK, NTRS, STT all firing at once, correlated exposure if the group rolls over
watch: Don't stack full size in all three custody banks at once -- pick the cleanest one or two

picks.name: State Street Corporation
picks.thesis: Third leg of the custody-bank trio, at new highs with TV BUY confirmation, though RSI 70.3 is the most extended of the three.
strengths: RS at new highs, VCP confirmed
Group-wide earnings catalyst window (7-15 days) across the custody banks
risks: RSI 70.3 -- most extended of the BK/NTRS/STT group
Trailing EPS growth actually negative (-24.1%) -- forward-looking re-rating, not trailing strength
watch: If only taking one custody bank, BK or NTRS screen cleaner on trailing fundamentals

picks.name: Bank of America Corporation
picks.thesis: Money-center bank at new highs with real EPS growth (+24.4%) and the cheapest forward multiple of the group (11.7x), but RSI 74.5 and earnings in just 7 days argue for a WATCH tier rather than chasing here.
strengths: 11.7x forward P/E is the cheapest name in the financials group
EPS +24.4%, revenue +8.1%
risks: RSI 74.5 -- overbought short-term
Earnings in 7 days -- one of the tightest binary windows in the whole list
watch: Wait for the print or a pullback toward the 50-day before adding; don't chase into earnings at this RSI

picks.name: Fortinet, Inc.
picks.thesis: Highest-scored technology name in the scan (94.1) with a confirmed VCP and RS at new highs -- cybersecurity demand is the one pocket of software still working while the rest of software lags.
strengths: Score 94.1, RS at new highs, VCP confirmed
Revenue +20.1%, EPS +28.6%
risks: 46.3x forward P/E is a premium multiple
Earnings in 23 days

picks.name: Garmin Ltd.

strengths: TV STRONG BUY, unanimous buy-side (16/0/10)

Room to run -- 6.6% below the 52w high, unlike most of today's leaders sitting at 0%

Consumer-hardware demand is more cyclical than the software/financial names on this list

picks.name: F5, Inc.
picks.thesis: Application-delivery/security name confirming an RS new high with TV BUY backing, a steadier alternative to the more extended mega-cap tech names.

picks.name: Okta, Inc.
picks.thesis: Identity-security name at new highs with real growth (EPS +21.2%, revenue +11.2%) but RSI 84.1 is the most overbought reading among today's tech picks -- TV still says BUY, but this is a WATCH, not a chase.
strengths: RS at new high, TV BUY (15/5/6)
Revenue +11.2%, EPS +21.2%

picks.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
picks.thesis: Score 93.5, RS at new highs, VCP confirmed -- one of the cleanest defensive-growth hybrids in the scan with real revenue acceleration (+26.9%).

RS at new highs

picks.name: Caterpillar Inc.
picks.thesis: Quant score is a strong 92.1 (EPS +30.2%, revenue +22.2%, VCP confirmed) -- but TradingView's independent technical read is an outright SELL (6 buy/11 sell), the sharpest quant-vs-TV disagreement of any pick on this list.

picks.name: Sirius XM Holdings
picks.thesis: TV confirms with a BUY (15/5/6) and the setup is real, but RSI 89.2 is the single most overbought reading in today's entire scan -- this is a WATCH for a pullback, not a fresh chase.
strengths: EPS +21.8%, cheap at 9.1x forward earnings

picks.name: IAC Inc.

Score of 92.6 is high on the quant scan alone
risks: No TV cross-check available -- size conservatively without a second opinion
RSI 72.9 -- already somewhat extended
Revenue growth -12.2% -- a holding-company/portfolio-restructuring story, not organic growth

picks.name: Kinder Morgan, Inc.
